fields.value: IBM
Forward Deployed Units (FDUs) - six-person pods with AI agents
Business domain specialists, architects, engineers, plus digital workforce of AI agents
Bridging the gap between AI pilots and production deployments at scale

value: 6-person pod can do the work of a 30-person team at materially better economics
Progress measured in working systems rather than slide decks
First named deployments moving AI from isolated pilots into production at scale

evidence.quote: IBM Consulting launched Forward Deployed Units (FDUs) yesterday, a service-delivery model built around six-person pods that combine domain specialists, architects, and engineers with a “digital workforce” of specialized AI agents handling coding, evaluation, testing, and documentation.

Each FDU brings together three skill types. Business domain specialists who rethink the underlying processes rather than just automating broken workflows. Architects who connect business strategy to executable system design. Engineers who actually build and scale the production systems. The digital workforce in the middle handles the long tail of coding, evaluation, testing, and documentation work that would traditionally require many more humans.
The FDU model is IBM’s answer to the most-discussed problem in enterprise AI deployment in 2026: the gap between AI pilots that look great in demos and AI deployments that actually run in production.

IBM Forward Deployed Units claim a 6-person pod can do the work of a 30-person team at materially better economics, with progress measured in working systems rather than slide decks. The first named deployments — Riyadh Air, Nestlé, Heineken, and Pearson — are already moving AI from isolated pilots into production at scale

fields.value: Anthropic
Insurance underwriting that took 10 weeks; security work that took hours; stalled HR transformation program; COBOL codebase four times larger than scoped

Insurance underwriting now takes 10 days; security work now takes minutes; working HR prototype in one week, full application in under two months; mainframe modernization tracking on time and under budget

Joint Center of Excellence with training and certification program for 30,000 PwC professionals; Claude available in ChatPwC internal AI assistant; three active AI incubation pods in Finance, Supply Chain, and Deal Making

PwC professionals across U.S. teams expanding toward global workforce of hundreds of thousands; 30,000 US professionals to be trained and certified; engineering teams; deal teams; finance, supply chain, and deal making incubation pods

Most enterprises still running on systems and processes built for a pre-AI world; clients looking for ways to apply AI that are secure, responsible, and capable of delivering measurable outcomes in complex business environments
Claude Cowork connects to enterprise data through Anthropic's Model Context Protocol
Accuracy and auditability prioritized for regulated industries; compliance readiness for FDA and EMA standards; frontier performance and compliance readiness

value: Insurance underwriting cycles compressed from ten weeks to ten days
Cybersecurity incident response accelerated from hours to minutes
HR transformation: working prototype in one week, full application in under two months, now running thousands of daily transactions
Mainframe modernization: COBOL codebase four times larger than scoped, tracking on time and under budget

evidence.quote: Anthropic and PwC today announced an expansion of their strategic alliance, deepening how PwC uses Claude to build technology, execute deals, and reinvent enterprise functions for clients across every industry it serves.

PwC has been leading AI's expansion into the parts of the economy where accuracy and reliability are non-negotiable—financial services, healthcare, life sciences, cybersecurity—and the results are clear.
Insurance underwriting that took 10 weeks now takes 10 days. Security work that took hours now takes minutes.
The companies are establishing a joint Center of Excellence and a program to train and certify 30,000 PwC professionals on Claude.
PwC will roll out Claude Code and Cowork starting with U.S. teams and expanding toward a global workforce of hundreds of thousands of professionals.
Most enterprises are still running on systems and processes built for a pre-AI world—a drag that is estimated to be more than $2 trillion.

Claude Cowork will help extend this reach into the broader workforce, running directly within tools such as spreadsheet, word processing, and presentation programs and connecting to enterprise data through Anthropic's Model Context Protocol.
It's starting with regulated industries—banking, insurance, healthcare—where accuracy and auditability matter most.
Across the production deployments below, clients are reporting delivery improvements of up to 70%.

claims.object: Rust port of Bun shipped in Claude Code

evidence.quote: Jarred Sumner, co-founder of Bun and Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic, used Claude Code to migrate Bun from Zig to Rust . A million lines of code were produced in less than two weeks, with 100% of Bun's existing test suite passing in CI before merge.
Mike Krieger, co-lead of Anthropic Labs, migrated a Python codebase to 165,000 lines of TypeScript over a weekend.
The Rust port was shipped inside Claude Code in June.

gate_results.detail: all proposal numbers occur in evidence

evidence.quote: Thinking Machines Lab, the startup founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, has released Inkling, an open-weights model with 975 billion parameters.

gate_results.detail: all proposal numbers occur in evidence
